_evidence: BYB-01 Bybit live reconciliation — milestone v1.7 phase 67 checkpoint 67-04

_history.run2: PostgREST 1000-row truncation + 7d fills retention clamp -> PR #575
_history.run3: funding cursor bug (trades cursor reuse, backfill never fired) -> PR #576
_history.run4: BYB-02 discovered: 8h match_key bucket collapsed sub-8h settlements, >50% funding rows silently dropped (~32k USD/180d) -> PR #577 (1h cadence + re-key migration + 365d backfill)

fills.provider_cap_note: Bybit /v5/execution/list retains only ~7d of raw fills (#563 provider cap, not a P&L bug); both the exchange and DB fills sides were clamped to effective_since for a like-for-like compare.

_interpretation.funding: CLEAN — 37,334 buckets exchange == DB, 0 missing, 0 extra, 0 days beyond 1e-9. BYB-01 funding parity holds after the BYB-02 1h-cadence fix + re-key + 365d backfill.
_interpretation.fills: db_count=0 is a DOCUMENTED artifact: Bybit /v5/execution/list retains ~7d (provider cap #563) and API-key strategies do not persist raw fills until trades ingestion (phase 70). Not a P&L bug — dailies derive from closed-PnL + funding.
_interpretation.dailies: 163/164 overlap days byte-exact at 1e-9. The single dirty day is the run date itself (2026-07-04, delta 4.57e-4): stored dailies derived at 16:32 UTC, harness recomputed at 16:47 UTC with ~15 min more intraday data — the anchor-to-today partial day legitimately moves. Timing artifact, not a discrepancy.
